Using it to shape your research questions and then verifying locally is a smart, efficient approach.' } ], u3l4: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'Marcus recorded a team meeting and got a transcript. Which task could AI help him do next?', options: [ 'Retroactively change what was said in the meeting', 'Turn the transcript into a clean set of meeting minutes with action items', 'Email the transcript to every person in the company automatically', 'Schedule a follow-up meeting on everyone\'s calendars' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI is excellent at taking raw transcripts and condensing them into structured meeting minutes, summaries, and action item lists.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Grace needs to prepare an agenda for a planning meeting. What should she give AI to get the most useful result?', options: [ 'Just say "write an agenda" with no other details', 'Provide the meeting\'s purpose, time limit, and the main topics to cover', 'Ask AI to decide what the meeting should be about', 'Give AI a list of everyone\'s home addresses' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'The more context you provide — meeting purpose, duration, and topics — the better the agenda AI will produce.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Ahmed takes rough notes in a meeting and wants to turn them into professional minutes. What does AI need from him to do this well?', options: [ 'Nothing — AI can create minutes without any notes', 'His rough notes, names of attendees, and the key decisions made', 'A recording of the entire meeting in video format', 'The official company template for meeting minutes' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI works best when you give it your rough notes, participant names, and key decisions. The more input you provide, the more accurate and useful the output.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Meeting minutes should clearly record what was ___, what decisions were made, and who is responsible for each ___ item.', blanks: ['discussed', 'action'], wordBank: ['discussed', 'action', 'forgotten', 'optional', 'ignored', 'colour'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'A good meeting ___ lists the topics to be covered in order, along with the ___ allowed for each item.', blanks: ['agenda', 'time'], wordBank: ['agenda', 'time', 'dinner', 'colour', 'opinion', 'budget'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the meeting-related tasks that AI can help with.', items: [ { text: 'Drafting a meeting agenda from bullet points you provide', correct: true }, { text: 'Summarising a meeting transcript into key points', correct: true }, { text: 'Attending the meeting and asking questions for you', correct: false }, { text: 'Listing action items with assigned owners', correct: true }, { text: 'Sending calendar invites to all participants', correct: false }, { text: 'Reformatting rough notes into a professional document', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things that should typically appear in meeting minutes.', items: [ { text: 'Date and time of the meeting', correct: true }, { text: 'Names of people who attended', correct: true }, { text: 'Everyone\'s home phone numbers', correct: false }, { text: 'Key decisions that were made', correct: true }, { text: 'Action items and who is responsible', correct: true }, { text: 'What each person ate for lunch', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What do you call the specific tasks assigned to people at the end of a meeting, with a person responsible for completing each one?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['action items', 'action item', 'tasks', 'next steps', 'follow-ups', 'follow up'], hint: 'These are the "to-dos" that come out of a meeting, each with a person\'s name attached.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Missing Minutes', prompt: 'Claire had a busy week and forgot to write up the minutes from Monday\'s team meeting. She has a page of scribbled notes. It is now Thursday and her manager wants the minutes today. How can AI help?', options: [ 'Claire should admit the minutes are lost and apologise', 'She can type her rough notes into AI and ask it to format them into professional meeting minutes', 'She should ask a colleague to write the minutes from memory', 'AI cannot help with handwritten notes, so she is out of luck' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Turning rough notes into professional meeting minutes is one of the most practical uses of AI for office work. Even messy notes can become a clean document quickly.' } ], u4l1: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'Ben asks an AI image generator for "a photo of a dog." The result looks odd. What is the most likely reason?', options: [ 'AI cannot generate images of animals', 'The prompt was too vague — more detail usually produces better results', 'Ben\'s internet connection was too slow', 'The AI made a random error that cannot be fixed' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI image generators need specific prompts. Adding details like breed, setting, lighting, and style will produce much more useful and accurate images.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which of the following prompts would most likely produce the best AI-generated image for a business flyer?', options: [ '"Make a flyer"', '"Nice picture for business"', '"A bright, welcoming photograph of a clean professional office with natural light, modern furniture, wide angle, photorealistic"', '"Office stuff please"' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Effective image prompts include the subject, setting, lighting, style, and mood. Vague prompts produce vague results.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Helen wants to use an AI-generated image in a commercial advertisement. What should she check first?', options: [ 'Whether the image looks good enough', 'The terms of service of the AI tool to understand ownership and usage rights', 'How long the image took to generate', 'Whether the image uses her favourite colour' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Commercial use rights for AI-generated images vary by tool. Always check the terms of service before using AI images in advertising or commercial material.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI creates images by learning patterns from ___ amounts of existing images, then generating new images based on your ___ description.', blanks: ['large', 'text'], wordBank: ['large', 'text', 'small', 'audio', 'random', 'video'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Adding ___ like the lighting, mood, and ___ to an image prompt helps AI produce more accurate results.', blanks: ['details', 'style'], wordBank: ['details', 'style', 'errors', 'nothing', 'delays', 'bugs'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the details that improve an AI image generation prompt.', items: [ { text: 'Describing the setting or background', correct: true }, { text: 'Specifying the lighting (bright, dim, golden hour)', correct: true }, { text: 'Mentioning the style (photorealistic, watercolour, cartoon)', correct: true }, { text: 'Typing in all capital letters', correct: false }, { text: 'Describing the mood or atmosphere', correct: true }, { text: 'Including the date and time you are writing the prompt', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the TRUE statements about AI image generation.', items: [ { text: 'More descriptive prompts generally produce better images', correct: true }, { text: 'AI image generators learn from large sets of existing images', correct: true }, { text: 'AI always generates perfect images on the first try', correct: false }, { text: 'You may need to refine your prompt and try again', correct: true }, { text: 'AI-generated images can always be used commercially for free', correct: false }, { text: 'Different AI tools can produce very different styles of images', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the word for the written description you give an AI image generator to tell it what image to create?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['prompt', 'description', 'instruction', 'command'], hint: 'It is the same word used for instructions given to any AI tool.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Flyer Photo', prompt: 'Gary runs a house cleaning business and wants a professional image for his marketing flyer. He tries asking AI for "a cleaning picture" but the results look generic and unappealing. What should he do?', options: [ 'Give up and use a stock photo website instead', 'Rewrite the prompt with more detail: for example, "a bright, cheerful photo of a sparkling clean modern kitchen, sunshine through the window, photorealistic"', 'Ask AI to just "make it better" without changing the prompt', 'Use the generic image anyway since it is free' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Specific, descriptive prompts dramatically improve AI image quality. Think about what you want the viewer to feel and add those details to your prompt.' } ], u4l2: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'Donna wants AI to write a blog post that sounds like her, not like a robot. What is the best way to achieve this?', options: [ 'Accept whatever AI writes and publish it without changes', 'Give AI examples of her previous writing and describe her style', 'Ask AI to write in the most formal style possible', 'Use AI for the first draft and then completely rewrite everything' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Showing AI examples of your writing and describing your style (casual, warm, direct, etc.) helps it match your voice much more closely.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Phil reads an AI-written draft and it sounds too stiff and corporate. What should he do?', options: [ 'Publish it anyway — most readers will not notice', 'Tell AI to rewrite it with a more conversational, friendly tone and show it an example of his writing', 'Delete the draft and write the whole thing himself', 'Ask AI to make it even more formal' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'You can guide AI to adjust tone by describing what you want and giving examples. Iteration is a normal part of working with AI on written content.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What does "writing voice" mean in the context of working with AI?', options: [ 'Using voice-to-text software to speak to the AI', 'The unique style, tone, and personality that comes through in someone\'s writing', 'How loud you speak when reading text aloud', 'A specific font that matches your brand' ], answer: 1, explanation: '"Voice" in writing refers to the personality and style that makes your writing sound distinctly like you — your word choices, rhythm, and tone.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'To help AI match your writing style, share ___ of your past writing and describe whether your tone is ___ or more formal.', blanks: ['examples', 'casual'], wordBank: ['examples', 'casual', 'errors', 'strict', 'passwords', 'formal'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'After AI produces a draft, you can ___ it to add your personal ___ and make it sound more like you.', blanks: ['edit', 'touch'], wordBank: ['edit', 'touch', 'delete', 'colour', 'ignore', 'print'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things you can tell AI to help it match your writing voice.', items: [ { text: 'Share a sample of your own writing as an example', correct: true }, { text: 'Describe your tone (warm, direct, humorous, professional)', correct: true }, { text: 'Tell AI your favourite food', correct: false }, { text: 'Mention words or phrases you commonly use', correct: true }, { text: 'Tell AI to avoid jargon if you prefer plain language', correct: true }, { text: 'Share your date of birth', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the TRUE statements about writing with your voice using AI.', items: [ { text: 'AI can mimic a style when given good examples', correct: true }, { text: 'You can edit AI output to make it sound more like you', correct: true }, { text: 'AI automatically knows your writing style without any guidance', correct: false }, { text: 'Describing your audience helps AI match the right tone', correct: true }, { text: 'Once AI writes something, you are not allowed to change it', correct: false }, { text: 'You may need to give AI a few tries before the voice feels right', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What do you call the process of going back and forth with AI — reading a draft, giving feedback, and asking for a revised version — to improve the result?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['iteration', 'iterating', 'refining', 'revision', 'editing', 'feedback loop'], hint: 'It means trying something, improving it, and trying again — a cycle of improvement.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Newsletter Problem', prompt: 'Sandra writes a friendly monthly newsletter for her small pottery studio. Her readers love her warm, chatty style. She tries using AI to write one issue but the result sounds cold and generic. Her readers notice immediately. What should she do next time?', options: [ 'Stop using AI entirely for the newsletter', 'Paste in two or three of her favourite past newsletters and ask AI to write in the same warm, chatty style', 'Ask AI to write it in a more formal, business-like tone', 'Let AI write it and just add a greeting at the top' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Giving AI concrete examples of your writing style is the most effective way to get output that sounds like you. It can then serve as a helpful starting draft that you personalise further.' } ], u4l3: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'Tom wants to use AI to brainstorm names for a new product line. What is the best approach?', options: [ 'Ask AI for one perfect name and go with it', 'Ask AI for 20 name ideas, then use those to spark his own creative thinking', 'Let AI make the final naming decision for him', 'Brainstorming is something AI cannot help with' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI is excellent at generating lots of ideas quickly. Use those ideas as raw material to inspire your own thinking, not as final answers.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Maria is stuck on a creative project and asks AI "What are some unusual approaches to this problem I might not have thought of?" This is a good prompt because:', options: [ 'It asks AI to solve the problem completely for her', 'It invites AI to offer fresh angles and push past the obvious ideas', 'It gives AI complete creative control', 'It is too vague and will not produce useful results' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Explicitly asking AI for unusual or unexpected ideas is a powerful brainstorming technique. It helps break out of familiar thinking patterns.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'James is working with AI to develop a business idea. The AI suggests something that is not quite right but sparks a new idea in his mind. This is an example of:', options: [ 'AI making a mistake that should be reported', 'AI failing to understand the task', 'Creative collaboration — an imperfect AI suggestion leading to a better human idea', 'Proof that AI is not useful for creative work' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Some of the best creative work with AI happens when an imperfect AI idea triggers a new direction in your own thinking. The collaboration itself is the value.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI can help you ___ by generating many options quickly, which you can then filter, combine, or use as ___ for your own ideas.', blanks: ['brainstorm', 'inspiration'], wordBank: ['brainstorm', 'inspiration', 'finalise', 'replacement', 'limit', 'invoice'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Good creative prompts often ask AI to think from a different ___ or to challenge your ___ assumptions about a problem.', blanks: ['angle', 'current'], wordBank: ['angle', 'current', 'colour', 'future', 'random', 'budget'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the ways AI can help with brainstorming and creative work.', items: [ { text: 'Generating a large number of ideas quickly', correct: true }, { text: 'Suggesting unexpected combinations of concepts', correct: true }, { text: 'Guaranteeing one idea will definitely succeed', correct: false }, { text: 'Asking "what if" questions to explore possibilities', correct: true }, { text: 'Making final creative decisions on your behalf', correct: false }, { text: 'Helping you push past an initial creative block', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the TRUE statements about building ideas with AI.', items: [ { text: 'You can build on AI ideas and improve them', correct: true }, { text: 'AI can help you see a problem from a new perspective', correct: true }, { text: 'AI\'s first idea is always the best one to use', correct: false }, { text: 'Combining your knowledge with AI suggestions often produces the best results', correct: true }, { text: 'Creative collaboration with AI replaces all need for human creativity', correct: false }, { text: 'You can ask AI follow-up questions to develop an idea further', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the name for the creative process of generating many possible ideas without judging them yet — just getting them out?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['brainstorming', 'brainstorm', 'ideation', 'idea generation'], hint: 'You might do this with a whiteboard and markers, writing down every idea no matter how wild.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Stuck Florist', prompt: 'Rachel owns a flower shop and wants to run a Valentine\'s Day promotion, but she has used the same ideas for years and feels completely stuck. Her friend suggests using AI to brainstorm. Rachel types: "Give me 15 creative Valentine\'s Day promotion ideas for a small flower shop." She gets a list back. Some ideas are obvious, two are interesting, and one sparks a completely new idea she had not considered. What should she do?', options: [ 'Use all 15 ideas exactly as written because AI produced them', 'Throw out the list because most ideas were not perfect', 'Focus on the two interesting ideas and the new spark of her own — develop those further, possibly asking AI follow-up questions', 'Ask AI to pick the single best idea for her to use' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'The goal of brainstorming with AI is not to find a perfect ready-made idea — it is to generate raw material that fuels your own creative thinking. Using the sparks is exactly the right approach.' } ], // ─── UNIT 5 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── u5l1: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'Tom asked an AI chatbot for a quote from a famous book. The AI gave him a confident, detailed quote — but it never existed. What is this called?', options: [ 'A software glitch', 'An AI hallucination', 'A copyright violation', 'A server error' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'When AI invents information that sounds real but is completely made up, it is called a hallucination. The AI is not lying on purpose — it is predicting text that seems plausible based on patterns.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Why do AI tools sometimes make up facts instead of saying "I don\'t know"?', options: [ 'They are designed to always give a confident answer', 'They search the internet in real time and misread results', 'They are trained to predict the next likely word, not to verify truth', 'They pull from a database of known lies' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'AI language models are built to predict likely text, not to check facts. This means they can produce text that sounds authoritative even when they have no reliable information on the topic.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Maria got an AI-written summary of a local news story with a name, date, and phone number included. What should she do before sharing it?', options: [ 'Share it immediately — AI is always accurate', 'Check the facts independently using a reliable source', 'Ask the AI to rewrite it with more detail', 'Trust it because it sounds professional' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Even well-written AI text can contain hallucinated names, dates, and contact details. Always verify specific facts using a trusted, independent source before sharing.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI hallucinations are convincing because the AI writes in a ___ tone, even when the information is completely ___.', blanks: ['confident', 'false'], wordBank: ['confident', 'false', 'boring', 'random', 'cautious', 'verified'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'To catch an AI hallucination, you should ___ important facts using a ___ source.', blanks: ['verify', 'trusted'], wordBank: ['verify', 'trusted', 'ignore', 'random', 'copy', 'entertaining'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap every statement that is TRUE about AI hallucinations.', items: [ { text: 'AI can invent quotes from real people', correct: true }, { text: 'AI always admits when it is guessing', correct: false }, { text: 'Hallucinated facts often sound completely believable', correct: true }, { text: 'AI hallucinations only happen with bad internet connections', correct: false }, { text: 'You should verify medical or legal AI answers before acting on them', correct: true }, { text: 'AI cannot hallucinate about recent events', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the types of content AI is most likely to hallucinate or get wrong.', items: [ { text: 'Specific statistics and percentages', correct: true }, { text: 'Common greetings and small talk', correct: false }, { text: 'Names of real but obscure people', correct: true }, { text: 'Bibliographic citations and book titles', correct: true }, { text: 'Simple arithmetic like 2 + 2', correct: false }, { text: 'Medical dosages and drug interactions', correct: true } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'James asked an AI for a doctor\'s phone number. The AI gave him one. What is the ONE thing James must do before calling that number?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['verify', 'check', 'confirm', 'look it up', 'verify it', 'check it', 'double check', 'double-check'], hint: 'Think about whether you should trust the number or _________ it first.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Confident Wrong Answer', prompt: 'Rachel asks an AI to summarize a court case from her town. The AI produces three paragraphs with judge names, case numbers, and a verdict — all written with total confidence. Rachel cannot find this case anywhere online. What is most likely happening?', options: [ 'The case is sealed and the AI has special access', 'The AI hallucinated the entire case using plausible-sounding details', 'The AI is pulling from a private legal database', 'The court records have been deleted from the internet' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'When AI generates highly specific details that cannot be verified anywhere else, hallucination is the most likely explanation. Confident, detailed output does not mean accurate output. Rachel should not share or rely on this information.' } ], u5l2: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'David wants to use an AI chatbot to help draft a letter about a health issue. Which piece of information is SAFEST to include?', options: [ 'His full name and date of birth', 'His health insurance account number', 'A general description of the health concern without personal identifiers', 'His home address and doctor\'s name' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'When using AI for sensitive topics, describe the situation generally. Avoid including your real name, ID numbers, account details, or other identifying information that could be exposed if the service has a data breach.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Linda typed her Social Security number into an AI chatbot to get help with a tax form. Why is this risky?', options: [ 'The AI will refuse to process government numbers', 'Your input may be stored by the AI service and could be exposed in a breach', 'The AI will automatically file taxes on your behalf', 'Social Security numbers confuse AI systems' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Text you type into AI services is often stored and used to improve the system. If the service is breached, your sensitive data could be exposed. Never type Social Security numbers, bank details, or passwords into a chatbot.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Carlos is using a free AI app he downloaded from the app store. He notices it asks for access to his contacts. What should he do?', options: [ 'Grant access — AI needs contacts to work properly', 'Deny access and ask why it needs contacts for an AI chat app', 'Uninstall all AI apps immediately', 'Give access only to business contacts' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'A general AI chatbot has no legitimate reason to access your contacts. Requests for unnecessary permissions are a warning sign of an app designed to harvest your data. Always question why an app needs each permission it requests.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'When using AI for help with a personal topic, use ___ descriptions instead of sharing your real name or ___ numbers.', blanks: ['general', 'account'], wordBank: ['general', 'account', 'exact', 'password', 'vague', 'random'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Text you type into an AI chatbot may be ___ by the company, so treat it like sending an ___ email.', blanks: ['stored', 'open'], wordBank: ['stored', 'open', 'deleted', 'private', 'encrypted', 'secure'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap every item that is UNSAFE to type into a public AI chatbot.', items: [ { text: 'Your Social Security number', correct: true }, { text: 'A general cooking question', correct: false }, { text: 'Your bank account password', correct: true }, { text: 'A question about local weather', correct: false }, { text: 'Your child\'s full name and school', correct: true }, { text: 'A request to explain a word', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the smart privacy habits when using AI tools.', items: [ { text: 'Use a separate email address to sign up for AI services', correct: true }, { text: 'Share your home address so the AI gives local results', correct: false }, { text: 'Read the app\'s privacy policy before using it', correct: true }, { text: 'Turn off chat history when available', correct: true }, { text: 'Re-use your main password on AI apps for convenience', correct: false }, { text: 'Avoid pasting confidential work documents into AI', correct: true } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'Sue wants to ask an AI to help her write a complaint letter about her bank. What should she use INSTEAD of her real account number?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['a fake number', 'made up number', 'placeholder', 'a placeholder', 'example number', 'fake account number', 'generic number', 'xxxx', 'placeholder number'], hint: 'She should swap it out for a ___________ or made-up number.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Helpful App', prompt: 'Patrick downloads a free AI assistant app. To get started, the app asks for his full legal name, date of birth, and home zip code. The app says this helps it "personalize your experience." Patrick has never heard of the company. What should he do?', options: [ 'Enter the details — personalization requires real info', 'Be cautious: this level of info is unnecessary for a chat app and may be data harvesting', 'Enter a fake birthday but use his real name and zip code', 'Only enter his zip code since that is public information anyway' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'An AI chatbot does not need your legal name or date of birth to work. Asking for these from a new, unknown company is a major red flag for data harvesting or identity fraud. Patrick should skip this app and find a reputable alternative.' } ], u5l3: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'Yuki receives an email from her "bank" with perfect grammar, no spelling errors, and a link to update her account. She did not expect this email. What is MOST concerning about this?', options: [ 'Banks never send emails', 'AI can now generate flawless phishing emails, removing the old "bad grammar" warning sign', 'The email is probably a newsletter', 'Perfect grammar means the email must be legitimate' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Scammers once made obvious spelling and grammar mistakes. AI writing tools now produce polished, professional-sounding scam emails that look indistinguishable from real ones. Good grammar is no longer a sign of legitimacy.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Marcus receives a voicemail that sounds exactly like his grandson asking for emergency money. He does not recognize the phone number. What could this be?', options: [ 'His grandson called from a friend\'s phone — it must be real', 'A deepfake voice clone created from audio collected online', 'A prank call with a voice changer toy', 'A telemarketer using a script' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI voice cloning can replicate someone\'s voice using just a few seconds of audio found on social media. This is used in "grandparent scams." Always hang up and call your relative back on their known number before sending any money.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Grace gets a video call where her "bank manager" appears on screen and asks her to confirm her PIN. The video looks real but something feels off. What should she do?', options: [ 'Confirm the PIN — the video proves it is real', 'End the call, then call the bank directly using the number on the back of her card', 'Ask the person to zoom in on their face', 'Check if the video quality is high definition' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Deepfake video technology can create convincing fake video calls. Banks will never ask for your PIN by phone or video. Always end the call and contact the institution directly using a number you look up yourself.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI-generated phishing emails are dangerous because they now have ___ grammar and can ___ the exact style of real companies.', blanks: ['perfect', 'copy'], wordBank: ['perfect', 'copy', 'poor', 'translate', 'random', 'improve'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'If you receive an urgent request for money or passwords, always ___ the contact using a ___ you already know and trust.', blanks: ['verify', 'number'], wordBank: ['verify', 'number', 'ignore', 'email', 'click', 'website'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap every sign that a message or call might be an AI-assisted scam.', items: [ { text: 'Urgent language: "Act now or lose your account"', correct: true }, { text: 'A request from a known contact from their usual address', correct: false }, { text: 'An unexpected request for gift cards as payment', correct: true }, { text: 'A voice that sounds just like a family member asking for money', correct: true }, { text: 'A newsletter you subscribed to last year', correct: false }, { text: 'A link that does not match the company\'s official website', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the actions that PROTECT you from AI-assisted fraud.', items: [ { text: 'Set up a family safe word to verify real emergencies by phone', correct: true }, { text: 'Click links in emails to check if they look real', correct: false }, { text: 'Call back using a number you find yourself, not the one provided', correct: true }, { text: 'Share less personal information publicly on social media', correct: true }, { text: 'Trust a message more if it has no spelling errors', correct: false }, { text: 'Report suspected scam messages to your email provider', correct: true } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'Ahmed\'s family wants to protect themselves from voice clone scams. What should they agree on in advance — a special word or phrase only family members know — to use when verifying a real emergency call?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['safe word', 'a safe word', 'code word', 'a code word', 'secret word', 'a secret word', 'password', 'family code', 'family password', 'verification word'], hint: 'This is sometimes called a family ___________ or code word.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Investment Opportunity', prompt: 'Claire gets a Facebook message from someone using her friend\'s name and photo. The message says her friend made $5,000 in one week using an AI trading app and shares a link to sign up. Her friend\'s profile looks slightly different than she remembers. What is most likely happening?', options: [ 'Her friend genuinely wants to share a great investment opportunity', 'This is likely an AI-cloned or hacked account used to run an investment scam', 'The friend set up a second account for business purposes', 'The AI trading app is real but only works for some people' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Scammers clone social media profiles and use AI to generate convincing messages promoting fake investment platforms. Red flags: unsolicited financial advice, pressure to click a link, and a profile that looks slightly different from the real one. Contact your friend directly through a known method before clicking anything.' } ], // ─── UNIT 6 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── u6l1: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'Ben wants to use an AI tool integrated directly into Microsoft Word and Outlook. Which AI assistant is BUILT INTO Microsoft 365?', options: [ 'ChatGPT', 'Gemini', 'Copilot', 'Claude' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Microsoft Copilot is built into Microsoft 365 apps including Word, Excel, Outlook, and Teams. It helps you draft documents, summarize emails, and analyze data without leaving the apps you already use.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Tom needs an AI assistant that can search the web in real time and summarize current news. Which of these is BEST suited for that?', options: [ 'Claude (with no plugins)', 'ChatGPT with Browse or Gemini with Google Search', 'An offline AI app with no internet', 'Any AI tool — they all have live web access' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'ChatGPT (with Browse enabled) and Gemini both connect to live web search, making them strong choices for current news or recent information. Claude and some other AI tools may have a knowledge cutoff and no real-time search by default.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Maria is choosing between AI tools for creative writing and wants one known for careful, nuanced responses with strong safety considerations. Which is often highlighted for this?', options: [ 'Copilot', 'Claude (by Anthropic)', 'Gemini', 'ChatGPT-3.5' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Claude, made by Anthropic, is widely recognized for thoughtful, nuanced, and safety-conscious responses. It is a popular choice for tasks like creative writing, analysis, and sensitive conversations.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Gemini is Google\'s AI assistant, which means it integrates naturally with ___ apps like Gmail and Google ___.', blanks: ['Google', 'Docs'], wordBank: ['Google', 'Docs', 'Microsoft', 'Apple', 'Excel', 'Teams'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'ChatGPT was one of the first widely available AI chatbots and is made by a company called ___. It is available in both ___ and paid versions.', blanks: ['OpenAI', 'free'], wordBank: ['OpenAI', 'free', 'Google', 'subscription', 'Microsoft', 'open-source'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap each statement that is TRUE about comparing AI tools.', items: [ { text: 'Different AI tools have different strengths and weaknesses', correct: true }, { text: 'All AI tools give identical answers to the same question', correct: false }, { text: 'Copilot is best for users already using Microsoft 365', correct: true }, { text: 'You must pick one AI tool and never try any others', correct: false }, { text: 'Gemini can draw on Google Search results for up-to-date information', correct: true }, { text: 'The most expensive AI tool is always the most accurate', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the factors that should influence which AI tool you choose.', items: [ { text: 'Which apps and services you already use', correct: true }, { text: 'The color scheme of the website', correct: false }, { text: 'Whether you need real-time web search', correct: true }, { text: 'The length of the company\'s name', correct: false }, { text: 'Your budget for paid subscriptions', correct: true }, { text: 'The specific task — writing, coding, research, etc.', correct: true } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'James uses Google Docs and Gmail every day. Which AI assistant would fit MOST naturally into his existing workflow?', placeholder: 'Type the name...', answers: ['gemini', 'google gemini', 'Gemini', 'Google Gemini'], hint: 'It is made by the same company that makes Gmail and Google Docs.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'Choosing the Right Tool', prompt: 'Rachel is a retired teacher who writes a community newsletter. She uses a Windows PC, subscribes to Microsoft 365, and wants AI help editing her writing and drafting emails. She has never used any AI before. Which tool should she try first?', options: [ 'A specialized coding AI — it handles all text tasks', 'Microsoft Copilot, already built into her Word and Outlook', 'She must learn three different AI tools before starting', 'She should wait for AI to become simpler before trying it' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Microsoft Copilot is built right into the Word and Outlook she already uses, making it the lowest-friction starting point. She does not need to learn a new app — Copilot appears inside her familiar tools.' } ], u6l2: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'David asks an AI: "Explain your reasoning step by step as you answer: should I renew my car lease or buy?" What technique is he using?', options: [ 'Few-shot prompting', 'Chain-of-thought prompting', 'Structured prompting', 'Role prompting' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Asking the AI to "explain your reasoning step by step" is chain-of-thought prompting. It encourages the AI to work through a problem logically rather than jumping to a conclusion, producing more accurate and trustworthy answers.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Linda wants to teach an AI her preferred writing style. She copies in two examples of her past writing and says "Write like this." What technique is she using?', options: [ 'Zero-shot prompting', 'Chain-of-thought prompting', 'Few-shot prompting', 'Structured output prompting' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Providing a few examples to show the AI what you want — before making your actual request — is called few-shot prompting. It is one of the most effective ways to get AI output that matches your personal style or format.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Carlos writes a prompt with clear sections: ROLE, CONTEXT, TASK, FORMAT, and CONSTRAINTS. What kind of prompting is this?', options: [ 'Casual prompting', 'Structured prompting', 'Random prompting', 'Chain-of-thought prompting' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Structured prompting organizes a prompt into labeled sections so the AI understands exactly who it should act as, what background information matters, what task it needs to do, and what format the output should take.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Chain-of-thought prompting improves AI answers by asking it to show its ___ before giving a final ___.', blanks: ['reasoning', 'answer'], wordBank: ['reasoning', 'answer', 'password', 'opinion', 'source code', 'feelings'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'When you give an AI two or three ___ to show the style or format you want, this is called ___ prompting.', blanks: ['examples', 'few-shot'], wordBank: ['examples', 'few-shot', 'questions', 'zero-shot', 'passwords', 'chain'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the prompting techniques that will likely improve the quality of an AI response.', items: [ { text: 'Telling the AI what role or expert to act as', correct: true }, { text: 'Using only one vague word in your prompt', correct: false }, { text: 'Asking the AI to think step by step', correct: true }, { text: 'Giving examples of the format you want', correct: true }, { text: 'Typing your request in all caps to "make it listen"', correct: false }, { text: 'Breaking a complex task into smaller sub-tasks', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap what you should INCLUDE in a structured prompt for best results.', items: [ { text: 'The role you want the AI to play (e.g., "Act as a financial advisor")', correct: true }, { text: 'Your social media password for authentication', correct: false }, { text: 'Relevant background context the AI needs', correct: true }, { text: 'The exact format you want the answer in', correct: true }, { text: 'A list of things the AI should NOT do', correct: true }, { text: 'Your full home address', correct: false } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'Sue wants the AI to help her write a formal business email, but her first attempt came out too casual. What simple word can she add to her prompt to ask the AI to explain WHY it made each writing choice?', placeholder: 'Type the word or phrase...', answers: ['explain', 'explain your reasoning', 'step by step', 'think step by step', 'show your reasoning', 'explain your choices', 'explain why'], hint: 'Tell the AI to ___________ its choices or reasoning.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'The Vague Prompt', prompt: 'Patrick asks an AI: "Write something about retirement." The AI produces a generic three-paragraph essay that is not useful to him. He wants a personal, upbeat Facebook post for his own retirement party announcement. What should he do to his prompt?', options: [ 'Ask the AI to "try harder"', 'Add role, context, format, and tone: "Write a friendly Facebook post for my retirement party this Saturday, in a warm and celebratory tone, under 100 words"', 'Switch to a completely different AI tool', 'Accept the generic essay and copy-paste it' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Vague prompts produce vague results. Adding specific details — the format (Facebook post), the context (retirement party), the tone (warm, celebratory), and a length limit — gives the AI what it needs to produce something genuinely useful.' } ], u6l3: [ // 3 quiz { type: 'quiz', question: 'Yuki is an accountant. She wants AI to help her explain a complex tax rule to a client who is not a financial expert. What should she ask the AI to do?', options: [ '"Write a technical report using accounting terminology"', '"Explain this tax rule in plain, simple language for someone with no financial background"', '"List all federal tax codes relevant to this topic"', '"Translate this into another language"' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Asking the AI to explain in plain language tailored to the audience is one of the most valuable professional uses. Yuki gives the AI the context it needs — the audience has no financial background — so the output will match the client\'s level.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Marcus is a nurse manager who wants AI to help him write a policy document. Which approach protects patient confidentiality?', options: [ 'Paste real patient case notes into the AI for examples', 'Describe the scenario in general terms without any patient details', 'Use the AI on a personal phone during lunch', 'Ask the AI to name the patients involved in policy examples' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Professional use of AI must respect privacy and compliance rules. Never paste real patient, client, or confidential employee data into a public AI chatbot. Describe scenarios generically to protect everyone involved.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Grace is a real estate agent who uses AI to draft property listings. After the AI writes a draft, what must she always do before publishing?', options: [ 'Publish immediately — AI listings are always accurate', 'Review and edit the draft to verify all facts, prices, and details are correct', 'Ask the AI to publish it directly to the MLS', 'Change the font before posting' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI is a drafting assistant, not a final authority. In any professional context, you must review AI output for accuracy. Facts like square footage, pricing, and legal disclosures must always be verified by the professional before publishing.' }, // 2 fill_blank { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'When using AI at work, never paste ___ client or patient data into a public chatbot. Always use ___ descriptions instead.', blanks: ['real', 'general'], wordBank: ['real', 'general', 'fake', 'exact', 'public', 'legal'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI can help professionals ___ routine documents faster, but a human expert must always ___ the final output.', blanks: ['draft', 'review'], wordBank: ['draft', 'review', 'delete', 'publish', 'copy', 'ignore'] }, // 2 tap_correct { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the tasks where AI provides STRONG professional value.', items: [ { text: 'Drafting a first version of a report or proposal', correct: true }, { text: 'Replacing the need for professional judgment entirely', correct: false }, { text: 'Summarizing a long document into key points', correct: true }, { text: 'Automatically signing legal contracts on your behalf', correct: false }, { text: 'Brainstorming ideas for a presentation or campaign', correct: true }, { text: 'Proofreading and improving the tone of an email', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap the smart habits for using AI in a professional setting.', items: [ { text: 'Check whether your employer has an official AI use policy', correct: true }, { text: 'Paste your company\'s internal strategy documents into a public AI', correct: false }, { text: 'Use AI to handle first drafts, then refine with your expertise', correct: true }, { text: 'Assume AI legal or medical advice is always correct', correct: false }, { text: 'Be transparent with clients when AI assisted your work, if required', correct: true }, { text: 'Use AI to speed up routine writing and research tasks', correct: true } ] }, // 1 type_answer { type: 'type_answer', question: 'Ahmed is a teacher who wants to use AI to create a quiz for his students. He already has a good idea of the topics. What is the FIRST thing he should give the AI so it creates a relevant quiz?', placeholder: 'Type your answer...', answers: ['the topic', 'a topic', 'context', 'the subject', 'subject', 'the lesson topic', 'lesson topic', 'the topic or subject', 'background information', 'the curriculum topic'], hint: 'He needs to tell the AI the ___________ or subject matter first.' }, // 1 scenario { type: 'scenario', title: 'The HR Manager\'s Dilemma', prompt: 'Claire is an HR manager who needs to write a performance improvement plan for an employee. She wants to use AI to help structure the document. She is about to paste the employee\'s full name, personnel file, and disciplinary history into ChatGPT. What should she do instead?', options: [ 'Paste everything — ChatGPT is a secure HR platform', 'Ask AI to generate a general PIP template using a fictional example, then fill in the real details herself offline', 'Write the document entirely by hand to avoid AI', 'Email the employee\'s file to the AI company for processing' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Pasting real employee data into a public AI chatbot violates employee privacy and potentially your company\'s HR compliance policies. The smart approach: ask AI for a template or structure using a generic scenario, then Claire completes the sensitive details herself in a secure environment.' } ], // ── UNIT 7 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── u7l1: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is the most important role of a thesis statement in an essay?', options: [ 'To fill the required word count', 'To state the main argument the essay will support', 'To list every source you plan to use', 'To summarise the conclusion before writing it' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'A thesis statement tells the reader the central claim or argument your essay will develop and support throughout.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'When using AI to help write an essay, which approach is most academically responsible?', options: [ 'Copy the AI output directly and submit it', 'Let AI write each paragraph and only change a few words', 'Use AI to brainstorm ideas, then write in your own words', 'Ask AI to rewrite your teacher\'s instructions as your essay' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'AI is a useful brainstorming and planning tool. Doing your own writing ensures the work reflects your understanding and meets academic standards.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which of the following best describes the "body" section of a standard essay?', options: [ 'One long paragraph covering every idea at once', 'A series of paragraphs, each developing a single supporting point', 'A restatement of the introduction word for word', 'A bulleted list of facts without explanation' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Body paragraphs each focus on one supporting point, with evidence and explanation linking back to the thesis.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'A strong essay introduction should hook the reader, provide ___, and end with a clear ___ statement.', blanks: ['context', 'thesis'], wordBank: ['context', 'thesis', 'bibliography', 'footnote', 'summary'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'When you ask AI to help outline your essay, you are using it as a ___ tool rather than letting it ___ for you.', blanks: ['planning', 'write'], wordBank: ['planning', 'write', 'editing', 'submit', 'grade'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all items that are good uses of AI when writing an academic essay.', items: [ { text: 'Generating an outline to organise your ideas', correct: true }, { text: 'Asking AI to explain a concept you find confusing', correct: true }, { text: 'Submitting AI-written paragraphs as your own work', correct: false }, { text: 'Getting feedback on your draft\'s clarity', correct: true }, { text: 'Having AI invent statistics for your argument', correct: false }, { text: 'Using AI to check your grammar and sentence flow', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the standard structural parts of a five-paragraph essay.', items: [ { text: 'Introduction', correct: true }, { text: 'Table of contents', correct: false }, { text: 'Body paragraph 1', correct: true }, { text: 'Body paragraph 2', correct: true }, { text: 'Body paragraph 3', correct: true }, { text: 'Conclusion', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the term for the final paragraph of an essay that restates the main argument and closes the discussion?', placeholder: 'Type the term...', answers: ['conclusion'], hint: 'It comes after all the body paragraphs.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Margaret\'s Assignment', prompt: 'Margaret is writing a 500-word essay on climate change. She asks an AI chatbot to "write a 500-word essay on climate change" and pastes the result straight into her submission. What is the main problem with this?', options: [ 'The essay will be too long', 'She has not done her own thinking or learning, and may violate academic integrity rules', 'AI cannot write about science topics', 'The essay will have no paragraphs' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Submitting AI-generated text as your own work bypasses the learning process and typically violates academic integrity policies, even if the text looks polished.' } ], u7l2: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which type of source is generally considered most reliable for academic research?', options: [ 'A personal blog post', 'A peer-reviewed journal article', 'A social media thread', 'An anonymous forum reply' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Peer-reviewed journal articles are checked by expert researchers before publication, making them among the most credible academic sources.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What should you do if an AI tool gives you a citation that you cannot find anywhere online?', options: [ 'Trust it and use it — AI does not make things up', 'Assume it is correct because the title sounds real', 'Verify it independently; AI can fabricate plausible-sounding but fake references', 'Ask the AI to try a different font for the reference' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'AI tools can "hallucinate" citations — inventing author names, titles, and journal names that look real but do not exist. Always verify every reference.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What does the acronym "DOI" stand for in academic citations?', options: [ 'Document of Information', 'Digital Object Identifier', 'Data Output Index', 'Directory of Institutions' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'A Digital Object Identifier is a permanent link attached to a published article, making it easy to locate the original source reliably.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI can help you find research ___, but you must always ___ that the sources actually exist.', blanks: ['leads', 'verify'], wordBank: ['leads', 'verify', 'delete', 'copy', 'ignore'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'A ___ is a list of all sources you used, placed at the end of your essay, while a ___ appears as a note at the bottom of the page.', blanks: ['bibliography', 'footnote'], wordBank: ['bibliography', 'footnote', 'thesis', 'abstract', 'index'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the sources that are generally reliable for academic research.', items: [ { text: 'Government health department website', correct: true }, { text: 'Peer-reviewed medical journal', correct: true }, { text: 'Random Wikipedia edit from yesterday', correct: false }, { text: 'University library database', correct: true }, { text: 'Unverified celebrity Twitter post', correct: false }, { text: 'Published textbook from a known university press', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things you should check when evaluating an online source.', items: [ { text: 'Who wrote or published it', correct: true }, { text: 'When it was published or last updated', correct: true }, { text: 'Whether the website has a nice colour scheme', correct: false }, { text: 'Whether it is based on evidence', correct: true }, { text: 'Whether other credible sources agree', correct: true }, { text: 'Whether the URL uses all capital letters', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What word describes a false or invented source that an AI presents as if it were real?', placeholder: 'Type the term...', answers: ['hallucination', 'hallucinated'], hint: 'It is the same word used to describe seeing things that are not there.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Derek\'s Research Problem', prompt: 'Derek asks an AI assistant to list five academic sources on nutrition and aging. The AI produces five titles with author names and journal names. Derek pastes them straight into his reference list without checking. Two of the sources do not exist. What should Derek have done?', options: [ 'Asked the AI for ten sources instead of five', 'Searched for each source in a library database or Google Scholar to confirm it exists', 'Assumed the AI was correct because it sounded confident', 'Used a different AI tool that produces longer titles' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Every AI-generated citation must be verified in a real database. AI can produce convincing but entirely fictional references.' } ], u7l3: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'How can AI most usefully help you create flashcards for studying?', options: [ 'By printing physical cards for you', 'By generating question-and-answer pairs from your notes or a topic', 'By sitting the exam on your behalf', 'By choosing which subjects you should study' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI can quickly turn your notes or a topic description into question-and-answer flashcard pairs, saving you time and covering more ground.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What study technique involves testing yourself repeatedly rather than just re-reading notes?', options: [ 'Passive highlighting', 'Speed reading', 'Active recall', 'Colour coding' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Active recall — testing yourself on material — is proven to strengthen memory far more effectively than simply re-reading or highlighting.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'If you ask AI to quiz you on a topic and it gives a wrong answer in the explanation, what should you do?', options: [ 'Accept it — AI is always accurate', 'Cross-reference with your textbook or a reliable source to check', 'Stop using AI for studying entirely', 'Report your teacher to the AI company' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI can make factual errors. Always cross-check important facts with your course materials or trusted references.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Spacing out your study sessions over several days, known as ___ practice, helps information move into ___ memory.', blanks: ['spaced', 'long-term'], wordBank: ['spaced', 'long-term', 'cramming', 'short-term', 'random'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'You can ask AI to create a ___ quiz on any topic, and then check your answers against your ___ materials.', blanks: ['practice', 'course'], wordBank: ['practice', 'course', 'live', 'graded', 'secret'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the AI-assisted study strategies that are genuinely helpful.', items: [ { text: 'Asking AI to explain a difficult concept in simpler words', correct: true }, { text: 'Having AI generate practice exam questions', correct: true }, { text: 'Letting AI sit your final assessment', correct: false }, { text: 'Using AI to summarise a long chapter into key points', correct: true }, { text: 'Asking AI to create a study schedule', correct: true }, { text: 'Copying AI summaries word for word into your answers without understanding them', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the items that describe effective revision habits (with or without AI).', items: [ { text: 'Testing yourself regularly on the material', correct: true }, { text: 'Reviewing notes the night before with no prior practice', correct: false }, { text: 'Breaking study into shorter, focused sessions', correct: true }, { text: 'Teaching the concept to someone else or out loud', correct: true }, { text: 'Re-reading the same page ten times without questions', correct: false }, { text: 'Mixing up different topics in one session (interleaving)', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the common term for digital study cards with a question on one side and the answer on the other?', placeholder: 'Type the term...', answers: ['flashcards', 'flash cards'], hint: 'They flash a question at you and you try to recall the answer.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Patricia\'s Revision Plan', prompt: 'Patricia has a history exam in two weeks. She asks an AI to create 20 practice questions on World War II, then checks her answers against her textbook. She also asks AI to explain any answers she got wrong. Is this a good approach?', options: [ 'No — she should only use the textbook, not AI', 'Yes — she is using AI to generate practice and then verifying with reliable sources', 'No — 20 questions is too many for AI to handle', 'Yes — but only if she submits the AI questions to her teacher for approval first' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Patricia is using AI as a practice-question generator and explainer while still relying on her textbook as the authoritative source. This is an excellent blended approach.' } ], u7l4: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is academic plagiarism?', options: [ 'Writing an essay that is too short', 'Presenting someone else\'s words or ideas as your own without credit', 'Using too many quotation marks in your essay', 'Choosing a topic that another student has written about' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Plagiarism means taking someone else\'s work — including AI-generated text — and submitting it as your own without proper acknowledgement.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Many universities now have specific policies about AI use in assessments. What is the safest first step for a student?', options: [ 'Assume AI is always allowed unless told otherwise', 'Use AI for everything and hope nobody notices', 'Read the course or institution policy carefully before using AI', 'Ask an AI chatbot what the university policy is' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Policies vary widely across institutions and even individual courses. Reading the policy first protects you from accidental violations.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which of the following is an example of ethically using AI in an academic context?', options: [ 'Having AI write your assignment and submitting it', 'Using AI to brainstorm, then writing the work yourself and disclosing AI use as required', 'Paying an AI service to sit an online exam for you', 'Feeding another student\'s essay to AI and asking it to reword it for you' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Using AI as a thinking aid while doing the intellectual work yourself, and declaring that use where required, is the ethical approach.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Submitting AI-generated text as your own is a form of academic ___, which can result in serious ___ consequences.', blanks: ['dishonesty', 'academic'], wordBank: ['dishonesty', 'academic', 'creativity', 'financial', 'technical'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'If your institution requires you to ___ AI use, you should note what tools you used and how they helped in your ___.', blanks: ['disclose', 'submission'], wordBank: ['disclose', 'submission', 'hide', 'invoice', 'decoration'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the actions that could be considered academic dishonesty.', items: [ { text: 'Copying a friend\'s essay and changing a few words', correct: true }, { text: 'Submitting a full AI-generated essay as your own work', correct: true }, { text: 'Using AI to explain a concept you don\'t understand', correct: false }, { text: 'Inventing quotations and attributing them to real authors', correct: true }, { text: 'Asking AI for feedback on your own draft', correct: false }, { text: 'Pasting AI content into your work without disclosure where required', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all statements that reflect responsible AI use in education.', items: [ { text: 'Checking your institution\'s AI policy before starting', correct: true }, { text: 'Using AI as a brainstorming partner, not a ghostwriter', correct: true }, { text: 'Submitting AI work and claiming you wrote every word', correct: false }, { text: 'Disclosing AI use when the course requires it', correct: true }, { text: 'Verifying AI-generated facts before including them', correct: true }, { text: 'Relying on AI because you find the subject boring', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the term for a statement at the end of a piece of work explaining what AI tools were used in its creation?', placeholder: 'Type the term...', answers: ['disclosure', 'ai disclosure', 'declaration'], hint: 'It is similar to the disclosure statement you sometimes see in financial documents.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Gerald\'s Dilemma', prompt: 'Gerald is struggling with an essay. He asks an AI chatbot to write a full draft, then changes about 15% of the words and submits it. His course policy says "AI may be used for planning only; written work must be your own." Has Gerald violated the policy?', options: [ 'No — he changed 15% of the words, so it counts as his own work', 'No — AI tools are free to use for any purpose', 'Yes — the policy clearly limits AI to planning; submitting an AI-written draft violates that', 'Yes — but only if the teacher uses plagiarism detection software' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'The policy specifically restricts AI to planning. Using AI to write the draft itself, regardless of how many words were changed, violates the stated policy.' } ], // ── UNIT 8 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── u8l1: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'What type of AI model is most commonly used to generate photorealistic images from text descriptions?', options: [ 'A decision tree', 'A diffusion model', 'A spreadsheet formula', 'A rule-based filter' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Diffusion models learn to remove noise from images step by step. When generating, they start from random noise and gradually shape it into a coherent image guided by the text description.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'How do AI image models learn what objects look like?', options: [ 'A team of artists draws millions of examples for them', 'They are shown billions of existing images paired with text labels during training', 'They search the internet every time you make a request', 'They copy images directly from photographers\' hard drives' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI image models are trained on very large datasets of image-text pairs. The model learns the statistical patterns that link descriptions to visual features.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What does the term "latent space" refer to in AI image generation?', options: [ 'The physical server room where the AI is stored', 'A compressed mathematical representation of image features the model has learned', 'The delay time between entering a prompt and seeing the image', 'The copyright status of the training data' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'A latent space is a high-dimensional mathematical space where the model encodes learned concepts. Navigating this space is how the model moves from a text prompt to a generated image.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'AI image generators start with random ___ and gradually refine it into a coherent image, a process called ___.', blanks: ['noise', 'diffusion'], wordBank: ['noise', 'diffusion', 'colour', 'compression', 'scanning'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'The quality and diversity of an AI image model depends heavily on its ___ data — the images it learned from during ___.', blanks: ['training', 'training'], wordBank: ['training', 'rendering', 'storage', 'uploading', 'printing'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all statements that are true about how AI image generation works.', items: [ { text: 'It uses patterns learned from millions of training images', correct: true }, { text: 'It copies images directly from the internet each time', correct: false }, { text: 'Diffusion models refine noise into images step by step', correct: true }, { text: 'The model understands the meaning of a prompt through language processing', correct: true }, { text: 'Every generated image is an exact copy of a training image', correct: false }, { text: 'The model can generate images it has never seen exactly before', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things that influence the output of an AI image generator.', items: [ { text: 'The text prompt you provide', correct: true }, { text: 'The model\'s training data', correct: true }, { text: 'The colour of your computer screen', correct: false }, { text: 'Style keywords in the prompt (e.g. "oil painting")', correct: true }, { text: 'Random seed or variation settings', correct: true }, { text: 'The time of day you generate the image', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the term for the process of training an AI image model, where noise is gradually removed to reveal an image?', placeholder: 'Type the process name...', answers: ['diffusion', 'diffusion model'], hint: 'It is also the name of the model type that uses this process.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Barbara\'s Curiosity', prompt: 'Barbara generates an image of "a cat sitting on a red chair" and the AI produces a realistic-looking result. Her friend says the AI must have stored that exact photo somewhere. What is the more accurate explanation?', options: [ 'Her friend is right — it pulled the exact image from a database', 'The AI created the image by applying patterns it learned from training data to match her description', 'Someone at the AI company drew it for her in real time', 'The AI searched Google Images and cropped a suitable photo' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'AI image generators do not retrieve stored photos. They synthesise new images by combining learned visual patterns guided by the text prompt.' } ], u8l2: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'In AI image generation, what is a "prompt"?', options: [ 'A keyboard shortcut for saving the image', 'The text description you type to tell the AI what image to create', 'The loading screen that appears before the image', 'A warning message from the AI system' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'A prompt is the written instruction you give the AI. The more clearly and specifically you describe what you want, the better the result tends to be.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which prompt is likely to produce a more specific and useful image?', options: [ '"A dog"', '"A golden retriever puppy playing on a sunny beach, watercolour style"', '"Something nice"', '"Make it look good"' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Specific prompts that include subject, setting, mood, and style give the AI much more to work with and produce more predictable, useful results.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is a "negative prompt" in AI image generation?', options: [ 'A prompt you type in capital letters', 'A description of things you do NOT want to appear in the image', 'A prompt that produces sad or dark images', 'An error message when your prompt is rejected' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Negative prompts tell the AI what to exclude — for example "no people, no text" — helping you avoid common unwanted elements in the output.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Adding a ___ keyword like "oil painting" or "pencil sketch" to your prompt strongly influences the visual ___ of the generated image.', blanks: ['style', 'appearance'], wordBank: ['style', 'appearance', 'colour', 'file size', 'resolution'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'A ___ prompt lists things you want excluded from the image, while a ___ prompt describes what you do want.', blanks: ['negative', 'positive'], wordBank: ['negative', 'positive', 'hidden', 'random', 'default'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the elements that can improve the quality of an AI image prompt.', items: [ { text: 'Specifying a visual style (e.g. "watercolour", "photorealistic")', correct: true }, { text: 'Describing the lighting (e.g. "golden hour sunlight")', correct: true }, { text: 'Typing your prompt in all capital letters', correct: false }, { text: 'Including the subject, setting, and mood', correct: true }, { text: 'Using a negative prompt to exclude unwanted elements', correct: true }, { text: 'Making the prompt as vague as possible', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the style keywords that are commonly used in AI image prompts.', items: [ { text: 'Photorealistic', correct: true }, { text: 'Oil painting', correct: true }, { text: 'Spreadsheet format', correct: false }, { text: 'Watercolour', correct: true }, { text: 'Comic book art', correct: true }, { text: 'PDF document', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the practice of carefully crafting and refining text instructions to get better AI outputs called?', placeholder: 'Type the term...', answers: ['prompt engineering', 'prompting'], hint: 'It involves engineering your instructions for maximum effectiveness.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Frank\'s Flyer', prompt: 'Frank wants an AI image for a community garden newsletter. He types "garden" and is disappointed with the generic result. His neighbour suggests adding more detail. Which revised prompt is most likely to give Frank what he needs?', options: [ '"GARDEN GARDEN GARDEN"', '"A colourful vegetable garden in summer with raised beds, warm sunlight, watercolour illustration style"', '"Something green"', '"Not a boring garden"' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'The detailed prompt specifies subject (vegetable garden), setting (raised beds), lighting (warm sunlight), and style (watercolour illustration), giving the AI clear direction.' } ], u8l3: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is DALL-E?', options: [ 'A voice assistant made by Apple', 'OpenAI\'s AI system for generating images from text prompts', 'A photo editing app for smartphones', 'A social media platform for sharing AI art' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'DALL-E is OpenAI\'s image-generation system. You type a text description and it produces an original image. It is accessible via ChatGPT and the OpenAI API.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which feature in ChatGPT allows you to generate images directly within a conversation?', options: [ 'The Advanced Data Analysis plugin', 'DALL-E image generation (available in ChatGPT with image capability)', 'The voice mode microphone button', 'The "Export to Word" option' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'When DALL-E integration is enabled in ChatGPT, you can ask it to create images in the same conversation where you are writing and asking questions.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is one key limitation of ChatGPT\'s knowledge when it does not have internet access?', options: [ 'It can only respond in English', 'It cannot discuss science topics', 'Its knowledge has a cut-off date and it may not know recent events', 'It requires you to log in every 5 minutes' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'ChatGPT\'s base model is trained up to a certain date. Without web browsing enabled, it does not have information about events after that cut-off.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'ChatGPT is made by ___, the same company that created the ___ image generation system.', blanks: ['OpenAI', 'DALL-E'], wordBank: ['OpenAI', 'DALL-E', 'Google', 'Midjourney', 'Apple'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'You can use ChatGPT for writing, answering questions, and ___, while DALL-E specialises in creating ___ from text.', blanks: ['summarising', 'images'], wordBank: ['summarising', 'images', 'printing', 'accounting', 'videos'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things ChatGPT can help you do.', items: [ { text: 'Draft an email', correct: true }, { text: 'Explain a complicated topic in simple terms', correct: true }, { text: 'Physically mail a letter for you', correct: false }, { text: 'Translate text into another language', correct: true }, { text: 'Generate an image via DALL-E integration', correct: true }, { text: 'Automatically update your bank account', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all statements that are true about DALL-E image generation.', items: [ { text: 'It creates original images from text descriptions', correct: true }, { text: 'You can generate images directly in ChatGPT', correct: true }, { text: 'It guarantees legally safe commercial use for all images', correct: false }, { text: 'You can iterate by refining your prompt if the first result is not right', correct: true }, { text: 'It always produces perfect hands and text in images', correct: false }, { text: 'It is made by OpenAI', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What is the name of the conversational AI assistant made by OpenAI that can also generate images using DALL-E?', placeholder: 'Type the name...', answers: ['chatgpt', 'chat gpt'], hint: 'It has "chat" in the name.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Helen\'s Project', prompt: 'Helen wants to create a simple illustrated recipe card. She is already using ChatGPT for writing help. A friend tells her she needs separate software to make images. Is the friend correct?', options: [ 'Yes — ChatGPT cannot do images at all', 'No — Helen can ask ChatGPT (with DALL-E) to generate an image without leaving the app', 'Yes — she must use Photoshop for any image work', 'No — but she needs to pay a separate fee to each OpenAI product' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'When DALL-E is available in ChatGPT, Helen can request an image directly in the conversation — no extra software needed.' } ], u8l4: [ { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is Midjourney primarily known for?', options: [ 'Writing long-form articles', 'Producing highly stylised, often artistic AI-generated images', 'Translating spoken language into text', 'Managing social media schedules' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Midjourney is an AI image generator celebrated for its artistic, often painterly or cinematic output. It is particularly popular with designers and creatives.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'Which company makes Adobe Firefly?', options: [ 'Google', 'Microsoft', 'Adobe', 'Meta' ], answer: 2, explanation: 'Adobe Firefly is Adobe\'s family of generative AI tools, integrated into products like Photoshop and Illustrator. Adobe emphasises it is trained on licensed content.' }, { type: 'quiz', question: 'What is a key advantage Adobe Firefly emphasises over some other AI image tools?', options: [ 'It produces images faster than any other tool', 'It is trained primarily on licensed and public-domain content, reducing copyright concerns', 'It is completely free with no usage limits', 'It can create videos as well as images' ], answer: 1, explanation: 'Adobe Firefly is designed with commercial use in mind and highlights that it is trained on Adobe Stock and openly licensed content, making it safer for professional projects.' }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Midjourney is accessed through ___, a messaging platform, while Adobe Firefly is built into Adobe\'s ___ suite.', blanks: ['Discord', 'Creative'], wordBank: ['Discord', 'Creative', 'Slack', 'Office', 'Twitter'] }, { type: 'fill_blank', sentence: 'Different AI image tools have different ___ styles and are suited to different creative ___.', blanks: ['aesthetic', 'purposes'], wordBank: ['aesthetic', 'purposes', 'spelling', 'prices', 'colours'] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all statements that correctly describe Midjourney.', items: [ { text: 'It is known for artistic, highly stylised image output', correct: true }, { text: 'It is accessed via Discord', correct: true }, { text: 'It is a product made by Adobe', correct: false }, { text: 'Users interact with it using slash commands and prompts', correct: true }, { text: 'It is primarily a word-processing tool', correct: false }, { text: 'It produces a variety of image variations for each prompt', correct: true } ] }, { type: 'tap_correct', question: 'Tap all the things Adobe Firefly can do.', items: [ { text: 'Generate images from text prompts', correct: true }, { text: 'Extend or fill in the edges of an existing photo (generative fill)', correct: true }, { text: 'Write legal contracts', correct: false }, { text: 'Apply AI-generated effects in Photoshop', correct: true }, { text: 'Replace parts of an image using text descriptions', correct: true }, { text: 'Print documents at the local library', correct: false } ] }, { type: 'type_answer', question: 'What platform do you need to join in order to use Midjourney for AI image generation?', placeholder: 'Type the platform name...', answers: ['discord'], hint: 'It is a popular chat platform used by gaming communities and creators.' }, { type: 'scenario', title: 'Rosemary\'s Design Choice', prompt: 'Rosemary is a small business owner creating marketing images. She is concerned about copyright and wants a tool where she can safely use AI-generated images in her advertising.
